1→# Agent Instructions
     2→
     3→This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get
     4→started, `bd prime` for session context recovery.
     5→
     6→## Quick Reference
     7→
     8→```bash
     9→bd ready              # Find available work
    10→bd show <id>          # View issue details
    11→bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work atomically
    12→bd close <id>         # Complete work
    13→bd prime              # Session context recovery (auto-called by hooks)
    14→bd dolt pull          # Pull beads updates from remote
    15→bd dolt push          # Push beads updates to remote
    16→```
    17→
    18→## Non-Interactive Shell Commands
    19→
    20→**ALWAYS use non-interactive flags** with file operations to avoid hanging on confirmation prompts.
    21→
    22→```bash
    23→cp -f source dest           # NOT: cp source dest
    24→mv -f source dest           # NOT: mv source dest
    25→rm -f file                  # NOT: rm file
    26→rm -rf directory            # NOT: rm -r directory
    27→```
    28→
    29→## Issue Title Guidelines
    30→
    31→Titles appear in `bd list`, `bd ready`, and terminal window titles where space
    32→is limited. The **first ~20 characters** must be meaningfully descriptive:
    33→
    34→| Bad | Good |
    35→|---|---|
    36→| `Add support for bookmark capture` | `Bookmark capture endpoint` |
    37→| `Update the search to use vectors` | `Vector search integration` |
    38→| `Fix bug where transcription fails` | `Transcription timeout fix` |
    39→
    40→- **Lead with the noun or area** ("Search ...", "Auth ...", "MCP ..."), not filler verbs
    41→- **Under 50 characters** — put detail in `--description`
    42→
    43→## Issue Types
    44→
    45→Use the correct issue type when creating issues:
    46→
    47→- `epic` — grouping container for related work. Not directly workable. Use `--waits-for-gate all-children`.
    48→- `feature` — delivers new user-visible or agent-visible capability
    49→- `task` — scaffold, chore, or infrastructure work
    50→- `bug` — something broken that needs fixing
    51→
    52→## Issue Slicing
    53→
    54→Break plans into beads issues that each deliver a complete, testable unit of
    55→value. Prefer thin end-to-end slices over layer-by-layer work.
    56→
    57→- Start from an observable outcome for a user, operator, or agent
    58→- Include every layer needed for that outcome to work in the same issue
    59→- Keep the slice small by narrowing scope, not by splitting DB/API/UI into separate issues
    60→- Use horizontal issues only for true shared infrastructure, and say what future slices they unlock
    61→
    62→Smells to avoid:
    63→
    64→- one issue for the database, another for the API, and a third for the frontend
    65→- issues that cannot be demoed independently after merge
    66→- titles named after implementation layers instead of capabilities
    67→
    68→Read `docs/policies/issue-slicing.md` before converting a plan into multiple
    69→issues.
    70→
    71→## Governance Gates
    72→
    73→Before closing or landing work, invoke the appropriate repo skills:
    74→
    75→- **Closure gate** (`scripts/ci/close-issue-check.sh`): automated pre-closure
    76→  validation — risk classification, doc checks, placeholder audit, test companion
    77→  check. Run before `bd close`.
    78→- **Ship gates** (`/ship-gates`): risk classification, quality gates, test/lint/build
    79→  verification. Required for all closures.
    80→- **Security review** (`/security-review`): hostile-input and trust-boundary review.
    81→  Required when changes touch auth, routers, uploads, storage, workers, providers,
    82→  or client token storage.
    83→
    84→See `.claude/skills/ship-gates/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/security-review/SKILL.md`.
    85→
    86→## Implementation Plans
    87→
    88→After plan mode approval, copy the plan into the repo:
    89→
    90→```bash
    91→cp -f ~/.claude/plans/<random-name>.md docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md
    92→```
    93→
    94→Reference from issues:
    95→
    96→```bash
    97→bd update <id> --notes="Plan: docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md"
    98→```
    99→
   100→## Completing an Issue
   101→
   102→An issue is not complete until:
   103→
   104→1. All code changes pass quality gates (tests, lint, build)
   105→2. Changes are committed
   106→3. The issue is closed with `bd close <id>`
   107→4. Changes are synced: `bd dolt pull && git push`
   108→
   109→## Git Workflow
   110→
   111→When work is complete and verified, merge directly into `main`. Only fall back
   112→to a pull request if there is an unresolvable merge conflict.
   113→
   114→### Commit messages and issue references
   115→
   116→When a commit includes changes to beads data, list all affected issue IDs:
   117→
   118→```
   119→Add bookmark capture endpoint and GraphQL schema
   120→
   121→Implements capture mutation with page fetch background job.
   122→
   123→Issues: flt-a1b, flt-c2d
   124→```
   125→
   126→### Worktree workflow
   127→
   128→When working in a git worktree, **always merge the branch back into `main`
   129→before ending the session.**
   130→
   131→**Before merging a worktree branch:**
   132→- Preview conflicts first: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>` then `git merge --abort`
   133→- Rebase long-lived worktrees onto main before merging
   134→
   135→**After merging a worktree that touches GraphQL schema files:**
   136→- Always regenerate: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`
   137→
   138→**Worktree cleanup — do NOT do it manually:**
   139→Claude Code manages worktree lifecycle automatically when invoked with
   140→`--worktree`. **Never** run `git worktree remove` yourself.
   141→
   142→## Session Completion Protocol
   143→
   144→When ending a work session, complete ALL steps below:
   145→
   146→1. **File issues** for remaining work (`bd create`)
   147→2. **Run quality gates** if code changed (`/ship-gates`)
   148→3. **Update issue status** — close finished work, update in-progress items
   149→4. **Sync and push**:
   150→   ```bash
   151→   git pull --rebase
   152→   bd dolt pull
   153→   git push
   154→   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   155→   ```
   156→5. **Verify** — all changes committed AND pushed
   157→6. **Hand off** — provide context for next session

   240→		var id uuid.UUID

1→package mcp
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"net/http"
     6→
     7→	mcpgo "github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go/mcp"
     8→	"github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go/server"

    11→	"github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/version"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// NewHandler creates an MCP HTTP handler with search, browse, and capture tools.
    15→func NewHandler(deps Deps) http.Handler {
    16→	s := server.NewMCPServer(
    17→		"flotsam",
    18→		version.Version,
    19→		server.WithToolCapabilities(false),
    20→	)
    21→
    22→	s.AddTool(searchTool(), searchHandler(deps))
    23→	s.AddTool(browseTool(), browseHandler(deps))
    24→	s.AddTool(captureTool(), captureHandler(deps))
    25→
    26→	httpServer := server.NewStreamableHTTPServer(s,
    27→		server.WithStateLess(true),
    28→		server.WithHTTPContextFunc(func(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request) context.Context {
    29→			if claims := auth.GetClaims(r.Context()); claims != nil {
    30→				ctx = auth.WithClaims(ctx, claims)
    31→			}
    32→			if mcpClient := auth.GetMCPClient(r.Context()); mcpClient != nil {
    33→				ctx = auth.WithMCPClient(ctx, mcpClient)
    34→			}
    35→			return ctx
    36→		}),
    37→	)
    38→
    39→	return httpServer
    40→}
    41→
    42→// requireAuth extracts claims from context, returning an error result if unauthenticated.
    43→func requireAuth(ctx context.Context) (*auth.Claims, *mcpgo.CallToolResult) {
    44→	claims := auth.GetClaims(ctx)
    45→	if claims == nil {
    46→		return nil, mcpgo.NewToolResultError("authentication required")
    47→	}
    48→	return claims, nil
    49→}

     2→import { Container, Title, TextInput, Stack, Text, Group, Chip, Button, Loader } from '@mantine/core'

    19→interface SearchData {

    22→    total: number
    23→    hasMore: boolean
    24→  }
    25→}
    26→
    27→const SearchQuery = graphql(`
    28→  query Search($query: String!, $limit: Int, $filter: ItemFilter) {
    29→    search(query: $query, limit: $limit, filter: $filter) {
    30→      items {
    31→        id
    32→        type
    33→        title
    34→        contentText
    35→        tags
    36→        source
    37→        sourceUrl
    38→        capturedAt
    39→      }
    40→      total
    41→      hasMore
    42→    }
    43→  }
    44→`)
    45→
    46→const TYPE_OPTIONS = [
    47→  { value: 'BOOKMARK', label: 'Bookmarks' },
    48→  { value: 'TEXT_NOTE', label: 'Notes' },
    49→  { value: 'VOICE_NOTE', label: 'Voice' },
    50→]
    51→
    52→export function Search() {
    53→  const [query, setQuery] = useState('')
    54→  const [submitted, setSubmitted] = useState('')
    55→  const [selectedTypes, setSelectedTypes] = useState<string[]>([])
    56→  const [selectedTags, setSelectedTags] = useState<string[]>([])
    57→  const [limit, setLimit] = useState(20)
    58→
    59→  const filter = useMemo(() => {
    60→    const f: Record<string, unknown> = {}
    61→    if (selectedTypes.length > 0) f.types = selectedTypes
    62→    if (selectedTags.length > 0) f.tags = selectedTags
    63→    return Object.keys(f).length > 0 ? f : undefined
    64→  }, [selectedTypes, selectedTags])
    65→
    66→  const [result] = useQuery<SearchData>({
    67→    query: SearchQuery,
    68→    variables: { query: submitted, limit, filter },
    69→    pause: !submitted,
    70→  })
    71→
    72→  const items = result.data?.search.items ?? []
    73→  const hasMore = result.data?.search.hasMore ?? false
    74→
    75→  // Collect unique tags from results for tag filtering
    76→  const availableTags = useMemo(() => {
    77→    const resultItems = result.data?.search.items
    78→    if (!resultItems) return []
    79→    const tagSet = new Set<string>()
    80→    resultItems.forEach((item) => item.tags.forEach((tag) => tagSet.add(tag)))
    81→    return Array.from(tagSet).sort()
    82→  }, [result.data?.search.items])
    83→
    84→  function handleSubmit(e: React.FormEvent) {
    85→    e.preventDefault()
    86→    setLimit(20)
    87→    setSubmitted(query.trim())
    88→  }
    89→
    90→  function handleLoadMore() {
    91→    setLimit((prev) => prev + 20)
    92→  }
    93→
    94→  return (
    95→    <Container size="md" py="xl">
    96→      <Stack gap="lg">
    97→        <Title order={2}>Search</Title>
    98→
    99→        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
   100→          <TextInput
   101→            placeholder="Search your knowledge base..."
   102→            leftSection={<SearchIcon size={16} />}
   103→            value={query}
   104→            onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.currentTarget.value)}
   105→            size="md"
   106→          />
   107→        </form>
   108→
   109→        {/* Type filter */}
   110→        <Chip.Group multiple value={selectedTypes} onChange={setSelectedTypes}>
   111→          <Group gap="xs">
   112→            {TYPE_OPTIONS.map((opt) => (
   113→              <Chip key={opt.value} value={opt.value} size="xs" variant="outline">
   114→                {opt.label}
   115→              </Chip>
   116→            ))}
   117→          </Group>
   118→        </Chip.Group>
   119→
   120→        {/* Tag filter — shown when tags are available */}
   121→        {availableTags.length > 0 && (
   122→          <Chip.Group multiple value={selectedTags} onChange={setSelectedTags}>
   123→            <Group gap="xs">
   124→              {availableTags.map((tag) => (
   125→                <Chip key={tag} value={tag} size="xs" variant="outline" color="accent">
   126→                  {tag}
   127→                </Chip>
   128→              ))}
   129→            </Group>
   130→          </Chip.Group>
   131→        )}
   132→
   133→        {result.fetching && <Loader size="sm" />}
   134→
   135→        {result.error && (
   136→          <Text c="red" fz="sm">
   137→            {result.error.message}
   138→          </Text>
   139→        )}
   140→
   141→        {result.data && (
   142→          <Stack gap="sm">
   143→            <Text fz="sm" c="dimmed">
   144→              {result.data.search.total} result{result.data.search.total !== 1 ? 's' : ''}
   145→            </Text>
   146→
   147→            {items.map((item) => (
   148→              <ItemCard key={item.id} item={item} />
   149→            ))}
   150→
   151→            {hasMore && (
   152→              <Button variant="subtle" onClick={handleLoadMore} fullWidth>
   153→                Load more
   154→              </Button>
   155→            )}
   156→          </Stack>
   157→        )}
   158→
   159→        {submitted && !result.fetching && result.data?.search.total === 0 && (
   160→          <Text c="dimmed" ta="center" py="xl">
   161→            No results found.
   162→          </Text>
   163→        )}
   164→      </Stack>
   165→    </Container>
   166→  )
   167→}

---

## Flotsam Exploration Summary

Here's what's available for your demo specs:

### 1. Web Routes (web/src/App.tsx)

Routes defined:
- **Home** (`/`) - Public landing page with feature overview
- **Login** (`/login`) - Auth entry point
- **Register** (`/register`) - User signup
- **Search** (`/search`) - Protected; semantic + full-text search with type/tag filtering
- **Browse** (`/browse`) - Protected; paginated browse with sorting and filtering
- **ItemDetail** (`/items/:id`) - Protected; full item view with metadata, tags, source links
- **Capture** (`/capture`) - Protected; tabbed capture interface (Bookmark, Note, Voice)

All protected routes wrap with `<ProtectedRoute />` and require auth.

### 2. GraphQL Schema (api/graph/schema/)

**Core types:**
- `Item` - id, type, status, sensitivity, title, contentText, contentUrl, tags, metadata, autoMetadata, source, sourceUrl, createdAt, updatedAt, capturedAt
- `SearchResult` - items[], total, hasMore
- `User` - id, email, displayName, createdAt, updatedAt
- `AuthPayload` - token, user
- `MCPClient` - id, name, tools[], maxSensitivity, active, lastUsedAt, createdAt

**Enums:**
- `ItemType`: BOOKMARK, VOICE_NOTE, TEXT_NOTE, DRIVE_DOC, HIGHLIGHT, FILE
- `ItemStatus`: PENDING, PROCESSING, READY, ERROR
- `SensitivityLevel`: PUBLIC, NORMAL, SENSITIVE, PRIVATE
- `ItemSortField`: CREATED_AT, UPDATED_AT, CAPTURED_AT, TITLE
- `SortDirection`: ASC, DESC

**Key queries:**
- `item(id: ID!)` - Fetch single item
- `items(filter, limit, offset, sort)` - Browse all items
- `search(query, filter, limit, threshold)` - Semantic + FTS search
- `me` - Current user
- `mcpClients` - List MCP clients

**Key mutations:**
- `captureBookmark(input: BookmarkInput!)`
- `captureNote(input: NoteInput!)`
- `captureVoiceNote(input: VoiceNoteInput!)`
- `updateItem(id, input)`
- `deleteItem(id)`
- `updateSensitivity(id, sensitivity)`
- `tagItem(id, tags)`
- `register(input: RegisterInput!)`
- `login(input: LoginInput!)`
- `createMCPClient(input)`
- `deactivateMCPClient(id)`

### 3. MCP Server Routes/Tools (api/internal/mcp/)

Three tools exposed via HTTP MCP server:

1. **search** - Query knowledge base
   - Parameters: query (required), limit (1-50, default 10), type (bookmark|text_note|voice_note)
   - Tries semantic search first (via embeddings), falls back to full-text search
   - Filters by sensitivity level

2. **browse** - Get full item content by ID
   - Parameters: id (UUID, required)
   - Checks sensitivity permissions
   - Returns formatted item with all metadata

3. **capture** - Create new item
   - Parameters: url (for bookmarks) OR content (for notes), title, tags (comma-separated), sensitivity
   - Routes to CaptureBookmark or CaptureNote depending on input
   - Returns created item

All MCP tools require JWT auth and enforce permission/sensitivity checks via audit logging.

### 4. Web Voice Capture Page (web/src/pages/Capture.tsx)

Three tabs in single component:

**BookmarkTab:**
- URL input (required), title, tags
- Mutation: `CaptureBookmark`

**NoteTab:**
- Title, content (required), tags
- Mutation: `CaptureNote`

**VoiceTab:**
- Audio recorder with red record button, play/re-record controls
- `useAudioRecorder()` hook handles recording state (idle, recording, paused, stopped)
- Display duration in MM:SS format
- Upload via `uploadAudio()` to S3 after recording stops
- After upload, capture with `CaptureVoiceNote` (audioKey, title, tags, source='web')

### 5. Seed Data Setup (api/internal/testutil/seed.go)

The seed creates realistic demo data:

**Users (3):**
- ketan@example.com, test@example.com, admin@example.com
- Password: bcrypt hashed "testpassword123"

**Items per user (9 total):**
- **4 Bookmarks:** Distributed systems, Go perf optimization, Wikipedia KM, pgvector
- **2 Voice notes:** Q2 planning meeting recap, Book recommendation (Designing Data-Intensive Apps)
- **3 Text notes:** Sourdough recipe, Japan trip packing, Career goals 2026

All items have:
- Rich metadata (word_count, domain, audio duration, format, etc.)
- Auto-metadata (category, topics, sentiment, people extraction)
- Tags (topic/*, source/*, person/*, project/*)
- Source tracking (chrome_extension, web_app, android_app, mcp)
- Timestamps (scattered across past 25 days)

**MCP Clients (2):**
- Claude Desktop (search, browse only - no create/delete)
- Cursor (search only)
- Both use fixed key hash for reproducible testing

**Audit Log (6 entries):**
- Search, read, create, delete attempts (some approved, some denied)
- Spans 10 days

Seed is invoked via: `cd api && make seed` (requires DATABASE_URL)

### 6. AGENTS.md (Key Sections)

- **Issue workflow:** bd list/ready/show/claim/close commands
- **Issue types:** epic, feature, task, bug
- **Issue slicing:** Thin end-to-end horizontal slices preferred
- **Git workflow:** Merge directly to main, only PR for unresolvable conflicts
- **Worktree protocol:** Use Claude Code --worktree, never manual git worktree remove
- **Governance gates:** closure-gate, ship-gates, security-review skills required
- **Session completion:** File issues, run gates, sync/push, verify all committed

### Demo Readiness Assessment

**Available for demo:**
- Full capture pipeline (3 input types)
- Search & browse with filtering
- Semantic + FTS search (if embedder configured)
- Item detail view with metadata display
- MCP integration with 3 core tools

**Not yet visible:**
